name: Rufa'a ibn Shaddad
title: Commander of Tawwabin
shortDescription: A prominent Shia in Kufa and one of the main leaders of the Tawwabin uprising who was martyred after a fierce battle in Ayn al-Warda.
fullStory:
Rufa'a ibn Shaddad al-Bajali was a companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and a brave and prominent Shia in Kufa. Having missed helping the Imam on Ashura, he became one of the five main leaders of the Tawwabin group.

Alongside Sulayman ibn Surad and Musayyab ibn Najaba, Rufa'a made every effort to compensate for the Kufans' shortcoming in helping the Master of Martyrs. In the battle of Ayn al-Warda against the massive army of Sham, he was a senior commander.

When Sulayman and Musayyab were martyred, the flag of command fell to Abdullah ibn Wal, and after him, Rufa'a took the flag and attacked the heart of the enemy with Haidari bravery.

After the battle of Ayn al-Warda and the return of the survivors, Rufa'a later joined Mukhtar al-Thaqafi's uprising and finally, in one of the bloody battles in Kufa, attained his long-held desire: martyrdom in the path of avenging Hussain (AS).
quote: (His slogan in the uprising:) O avengers of Hussain!
